hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M156 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The Mercedes M156 is AMG's first clean-sheet V8 — a high-revving naturally aspirated icon, but head bolt and camshaft wear are well-documented weaknesses. Backed by real UK enquiries [MB-VERIFIED].

verdictRating.confidence: [X% confidence — based on [X] UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist rebuild reports]

rows.dimension: Based on recon cost vs lowest host value

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: "The M156 delivers thrilling naturally aspirated V8 character — but head bolt failure and camshaft wear can be ruinous without preventative work — buy only with documented head bolt and camshaft history."
verdictRating.bestFor: Enthusiasts seeking the last great naturally aspirated AMG V8 with proper maintenance records.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented evidence of head bolt update (2011+ models exempt) or camshaft/lifter inspection.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M156 typically takes 12–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [MB-QUOTE].

items.question: Cost — "How much does an M156 engine replacement cost?"
items.answer: A reconditioned M156 typically costs £5,500–£8,000 supply only [MB-QUOTE], varying by model variant and specification. Fitting adds £780–£1,700 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

items.question: Reliability — "Is the M156 a reliable engine?"
items.answer: [Answer to be completed based on Section 3 star rating and confidence basis.]

items.question: Common failure — "What is the most common M156 failure?"
items.answer: Head bolt stretching is the most critical failure — early M156 units (pre-2011) suffer from insufficient bolt strength, causing coolant leaks into cylinders and potential engine destruction. Camshaft and lifter wear is also widespread, with a tell-tale ticking noise on cold start.

items.question: Fitment — "Which Mercedes-Benz models use the M156?"
items.answer: The M156 powers AMG '63' models across the range, including the C63, E63, ML63, CLS63, S63, CL63, CLK63, and the rare R63 — fitted from approximately 2006 to 2015.

items.question: Longevity — "How long does the M156 last?"
items.answer: With proper maintenance and the head bolt update completed, M156 engines can exceed 150,000 miles. Without the head bolt fix and regular camshaft inspections, major failure is significantly more likely, often before 80,000 miles.
